other_observers: Brandon Percival, Brian Gibbons, Larry Semo, Mymm Ackley; I found and identified the bird; I do not know if it was seen by other independently.
duration: off and on for about 10 minutes
specific_location: In the large Russian Olive grove near "The Bell" east and a bit south of the banding station at the Holmes Grove, Chico Basin Ranch
description: A large and large-headed Empidonax flycatcher, the bird's bill was quite long (being at least as long as the distance between the base of the bill and the back of the eye) and wide and was black above and yellowish-orange below. The head was greenish-gray, contrasting somewhat with the greener back. The rump was the same color as the back and both were a bit brighter than olive. There were thin, but obvious, yellowish eye rings that were virtually complete. The grayish (with slight green cast) auriculars contrasted strongly and sharply with the white chin and throat.  The wings were blackish, with strong whitish-yellow wing bars and strong white fringes to the tertials. The primary projection was fairly long and was equal to about 40% of the length of the longest tertial. The tail was dark brown with pale outer webs to the outer rectrices.  The chest was white with vague grayish smudging. The rest of the underparts were whitish except that the flanks and some of the vent area were washed variably with a pale lemon yellow. The legs and eyes were dark.Behaviors: Foraging among Russian OlivesCall: Heard to call once, the bird gave a short, somewhat quiet, "peip"Plumage: Alternate
photographer: Photographer: Tony Leukering, Tony Leukering, Tony Leukering, Tony Leukering
